baseline.notes: Run 38b on Rush: F1 0.392, P 0.356, R 0.436. free_kick_shot was 88% FP (30/34); shot_stop_diving 85% FP (17/20). Sampling reasonings revealed the VLM uses near-identical template language for both TPs and FPs — confabulation. Run #41 tightens the VLM prompt to require explicit evidence (defensive wall for free kicks, visible shot for diving saves) and removes the 'if in doubt, emit' instruction that was actively pushing over-emission.

targets.notes: Aggressive precision targets for free_kick_shot (FPs 30 → ≤10) and shot_stop_diving (FPs 17 → ≤8). Acceptable to lose 2-3 TPs given the FP cuts available. F1 0.45 = ~+0.06 over baseline; would be the first run to break the 0.39 ceiling. If F1 drops below 0.38, prompt change is too aggressive — revert and iterate.

notes: Run #41 — VLM prompt tightening on Rush. Commit 5229ab9: free_kick_shot requires defensive wall OR foul context; shot_stop_diving requires visible shot + full dive + visible deflection; removed 'if in doubt, emit' bias from the 'before you conclude open play, check these' section. Also fixes the poller's shell-splitting bug on eval args (was breaking GT paths with spaces). Same gate config as Run #38b (ball chain linking + GK proximity + trajectory) so any F1 movement is attributable to the prompt change.
